strengths: Established proper traction with Maryland grasper before cutting
Maintained steady instrument control throughout the cutting phases
Demonstrated good coordination between both instruments during circle following
weaknesses: Failed to complete the circle within the time limit
Cutting pace was too slow for intermediate level performance
May have lost focus or momentum during the final portion of the circle

drills.name: Circle tracking quarter-cuts
drills.description: Cut the circle in four controlled quadrants while maintaining traction, focusing on consistent pace throughout each segment.
drills.targets_weakness: Failed to complete the circle within the time limit.

drills.name: Traction-angle practice
drills.description: 10 repetitions of repositioning the Maryland to improve scissor entry angle and cutting efficiency.
drills.targets_weakness: Cutting pace was too slow for intermediate level performance.

drills.name: Continuous cutting rhythm drill
drills.description: Practice maintaining steady cutting rhythm without pauses, completing partial circles in timed segments.
drills.targets_weakness: May have lost focus or momentum during the final portion of the circle.

progression_note: At the intermediate level, focus on converting your solid technique into faster execution. Your instrument control is good, but you need to maintain cutting momentum to complete tasks within time limits.
